title: Conroe ISD Board Meeting — April 21, 2026

items.title: Discuss Principal Candidate
items.context: The board will meet in closed session to discuss a candidate for a principal position. Personnel deliberations — including hiring — are legally permitted to occur in private under Texas Government Code §551.074 to protect the individuals involved.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item.

items.title: Update Pursuant to School Board Policy EHBAG (Local)
items.context: The board will receive a private legal briefing related to policy EHBAG (Local), which governs special programs. Consultation with the district's attorney in closed session is permitted under Texas Government Code §551.071 when the matter involves pending or contemplated litigation or a situation where attorney-client confidentiality applies.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

items.title: / III.A. Regarding Potential Sale of Property
items.context: The board will privately deliberate a potential sale of district-owned property, which is allowed under Texas Government Code §551.072 when open discussion could harm the district's negotiating position. Following the closed session, the board will reconvene publicly under Item III.A. to take any formal action on the matter.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

items.title: Consider and Approve Minutes
items.context: The board will approve the official minutes from its previous meeting(s), confirming the written record of prior proceedings is accurate.
items.history: Approval of meeting minutes is a routine item at every regular board meeting.

items.title: Consider and Approve Amendment to the Budget
items.context: The board is asked to approve a mid-year or periodic adjustment to the district's budget. Budget amendments are normal in school districts and reflect changes in revenues, expenses, or funding since the original budget was adopted.
items.history: Budget amendments are a recurring item at CISD board meetings throughout the fiscal year.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy DBD (Local) — Employment Requirements and Restrictions: Conflict of Interest
items.context: The board is reviewing proposed changes to policy DBD (Local), which governs rules around conflicts of interest for district employees. A "first reading" means the board is reviewing the proposed policy language for the first time before any vote to adopt it, which typically comes at a subsequent meeting.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific policy update based on provided records.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEA (Local) — Attendance: Compulsory Attendance
items.context: The board is reviewing proposed changes to policy FEA (Local), which sets out the district's rules regarding compulsory (required by law) student attendance. This is a first reading, meaning formal adoption will come at a later meeting after trustees have had time to review the language.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific policy update based on provided records.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEC (Local) — Attendance: Attendance for Credit
items.context: The board is reviewing proposed updates to policy FEC (Local), which governs how student attendance affects earning course credit. As with all first readings, no final vote will be taken at this meeting.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific policy update based on provided records.

items.title: Consider on First Reading Board Policy GC (Local) — Public Notices
items.context: The board is reviewing proposed changes to policy GC (Local), which governs how the district communicates public notices to the community. This is a first reading only; adoption would come at a future meeting.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific policy update based on provided records.

items.title: Receive Human Resources Report and Consider and Approve Employment of Professional Personnel
items.context: The board will receive a staffing update from the Human Resources department and vote to officially approve the hiring of professional staff members. This is a standard recurring item required by Texas law, as the board must formally approve employment of certain personnel.
items.history: HR reports and professional personnel approvals appear as regular consent agenda items at every CISD board meeting.

items.title: Receive Financial Reports
items.context: The board will review regular financial reports providing an update on the district's revenues, expenditures, and overall fiscal status. This is a routine transparency and oversight item required of Texas school boards.
items.history: Financial reports are a standing item on CISD's monthly board meeting agendas.

items.title: Approval of the TCEQ Required Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan for the Conroe ISD Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant Facility
items.context: The board will vote to approve a Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan required by the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) for the district's Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant Facility. Texas regulations require entities that operate water systems to maintain and periodically update these plans.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

items.title: Recognize Principal for DAEP/JJAEP
items.context: The board will formally recognize the principal serving the District Alternative Education Program (DAEP) and/or Juvenile Justice Alternative Education Program (JJAEP). These programs serve students who have been removed from traditional school settings, and this recognition highlights the leadership guiding those programs.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

items.title: Reconsider and Approve the Mascot for Cartwright Junior High
items.context: The board will revisit and take a formal vote on the official mascot for Cartwright Junior High School. The use of the word "reconsider" suggests this item may have been discussed or voted on previously and is being brought back for further action or a revised decision.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

items.title: Receive Information Regarding Senate Bill 546 Requirement for Bus Seat Belts
items.context: The board will receive a presentation or update on Senate Bill 546, a Texas law that may require the district to equip school buses with seat belts. This is an informational item to help the board understand the requirements, timeline, and potential cost implications for the district.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

items.title: Consider and Possibly Adopt District's Virtual School Plan
items.context: The board will consider formally adopting the district's plan for virtual (online) schooling. Texas law requires school districts to have an approved virtual school plan in place to offer or continue virtual learning options for students.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

items.title: Receive Information Regarding District Staffing Plan
items.context: The board will receive an informational presentation on the district's staffing plan, which may address teacher and staff hiring, allocation, and workforce needs for the coming school year. This is an informational item and does not require a vote at this meeting.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

items.title: Consider and Possibly Approve Library Materials in Accordance with Texas Education Code §33.026, School Board Policy EFB (Local)
items.context: The board will vote on approving library materials for district schools, following the process established by Texas Education Code §33.026 and local policy EFB. This process governs how the district reviews and selects books and other materials available to students in school libraries.
items.history: Library material approvals have become a recurring item at CISD board meetings, reflecting statewide legislative focus on school library content. No specific prior action details are available from the provided records.

items.title: Consider and Possibly Adopt District's Special Education Homecoming Project Plan
items.context: The board will consider approving a "Homecoming Project Plan" for Special Education, which appears to be an initiative supporting the reintegration or transition of special education students into general education settings or their home campuses. This type of plan is consistent with federal and state requirements to serve students in the least restrictive environment.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

items.title: Receive Information Regarding The Texas Standard Character Education Program
items.context: The board will receive an informational update on the Texas Standard Character Education Program, a state initiative that encourages schools to incorporate character development — such as integrity, responsibility, and citizenship — into their curricula and culture. No vote is required at this meeting.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

items.title: Receive 2026–2027 Budget Information
items.context: The board will receive an early presentation on the proposed budget for the 2026–2027 school year. This is an informational item that allows trustees to begin reviewing revenue projections, spending priorities, and any funding changes ahead of formal budget adoption later in the year.
items.history: Budget planning presentations are a recurring item in the spring months at CISD board meetings, as the district prepares to adopt a new annual budget before the start of the fiscal year.

items.title: Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for The Woodlands 9th Grade and College Park High School Renovations Project
items.context: The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk (CMR) — a procurement method where a contractor is hired early in the design process and guarantees a maximum price — for renovation work at The Woodlands 9th Grade campus and College Park High School. This is a significant spending decision related to facility improvements, likely connected to a voter-approved bond program.
items.history: Construction and renovation projects at multiple campuses have been an ongoing focus for CISD, consistent with bond program implementation. No specific prior action details for this project are available from the provided records.

items.title: Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for The Auxiliary Facility Renovations Project
items.context: The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk for renovations to an auxiliary district facility. The specific facility is not named in the agenda, but this follows the same CMR procurement process used for other capital projects.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific project based on provided records.

items.title: Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for the Caney Creek High School Renovation and District Safety and Security Upgrades Project
items.context: The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk for a combined project at Caney Creek High School that includes both facility renovations and district-wide safety and security upgrades. Security-related construction is a high-priority area for Texas school districts under state law.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific project based on provided records.

items.title: Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for the Anderson and Glen Loch Elementary School Additions and Renovations Project
items.context: The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk for addition and renovation work at Anderson Elementary and Glen Loch Elementary schools. This type of project may address capacity needs, aging infrastructure, or program space requirements at the campuses.
items.history: No prior history available for this specific project based on provided records.

items.title: Consider and Approve Annexation of San Jacinto Elementary Site into MUD 111
items.context: The board will vote on whether to annex the San Jacinto Elementary School property into Municipal Utility District (MUD) 111, and authorize the Superintendent to sign the necessary documents. Annexation into a MUD can affect how water, sewer, and other utility services are provided to the campus and may have financial implications for the district.

# Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2026-04-21
*Regular Meeting — Agenda Breakdown*
*Generated 2026-04-15 21:48 UTC*

---

# Conroe Independent School District — Board of Trustees
## Regular Meeting | Tuesday, April 21, 2026 | 5:00 PM
### Deane L. Sadler Administration Building | 3205 West Davis, Conroe, TX 77304
*[Join virtually](http://tiny.conroeisd.net/R78KV)*

---

## Procedural Items
The following items are standard meeting procedures and will not be broken down individually:
- **I.A.** Invocation
- **I.B.** Pledge of Allegiance
- **V.** Citizen Participation
- **XII.** Take Requests from Trustees Regarding Future Board Agenda Items
- **XIII.** Adjourn

---

## Closed Session (Section II)
*The board will meet privately before the public session. Closed sessions are permitted under the Texas Open Meetings Act for specific topics. All final votes must occur in open session.*

### II.A.1. Discuss Principal Candidate
**Context:** The board will meet in closed session to discuss a candidate for a principal position. Personnel deliberations — including hiring — are legally permitted to occur in private under Texas Government Code §551.074 to protect the individuals involved.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item.

---

### II.B.1. Update Pursuant to School Board Policy EHBAG (Local)
**Context:** The board will receive a private legal briefing related to policy EHBAG (Local), which governs special programs. Consultation with the district's attorney in closed session is permitted under Texas Government Code §551.071 when the matter involves pending or contemplated litigation or a situation where attorney-client confidentiality applies.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

---

### II.C.1. / III.A. Regarding Potential Sale of Property
**Context:** The board will privately deliberate a potential sale of district-owned property, which is allowed under Texas Government Code §551.072 when open discussion could harm the district's negotiating position. Following the closed session, the board will reconvene publicly under Item III.A. to take any formal action on the matter.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

---

## Special Recognition (Section IV)

### IV.A–D. UIL/THSPA and UIL State Championship Teams
**Context:** The board will formally recognize four CISD athletic teams for winning state championships: the Conroe High School, Grand Oaks High School, and The Woodlands High School powerlifting teams (UIL/THSPA Class 6A), and the College Park High School boys soccer team (UIL Class 6A). These recognitions are a standard part of board meetings to celebrate student achievement.

**Historical Summary:** Recognition of championship athletic teams is a recurring item at CISD board meetings. No specific prior history available from provided records.

---

## Consent Agenda (Section VI)
*Consent agenda items are typically voted on together as a package without individual discussion unless a trustee requests an item be pulled for separate consideration.*

### VI.A. Consider and Approve Minutes
**Context:** The board will approve the official minutes from its previous meeting(s), confirming the written record of prior proceedings is accurate.

**Historical Summary:** Approval of meeting minutes is a routine item at every regular board meeting.

---

### VI.B. Consider and Approve Amendment to the Budget
**Context:** The board is asked to approve a mid-year or periodic adjustment to the district's budget. Budget amendments are normal in school districts and reflect changes in revenues, expenses, or funding since the original budget was adopted.

**Historical Summary:** Budget amendments are a recurring item at CISD board meetings throughout the fiscal year.

---

### VI.C. Consider on First Reading Board Policy DBD (Local) — Employment Requirements and Restrictions: Conflict of Interest
**Context:** The board is reviewing proposed changes to policy DBD (Local), which governs rules around conflicts of interest for district employees. A "first reading" means the board is reviewing the proposed policy language for the first time before any vote to adopt it, which typically comes at a subsequent meeting.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific policy update based on provided records.

---

### VI.D. Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEA (Local) — Attendance: Compulsory Attendance
**Context:** The board is reviewing proposed changes to policy FEA (Local), which sets out the district's rules regarding compulsory (required by law) student attendance. This is a first reading, meaning formal adoption will come at a later meeting after trustees have had time to review the language.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific policy update based on provided records.

---

### VI.E. Consider on First Reading Board Policy FEC (Local) — Attendance: Attendance for Credit
**Context:** The board is reviewing proposed updates to policy FEC (Local), which governs how student attendance affects earning course credit. As with all first readings, no final vote will be taken at this meeting.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific policy update based on provided records.

---

### VI.F. Consider on First Reading Board Policy GC (Local) — Public Notices
**Context:** The board is reviewing proposed changes to policy GC (Local), which governs how the district communicates public notices to the community. This is a first reading only; adoption would come at a future meeting.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific policy update based on provided records.

---

### VI.G. Receive Human Resources Report and Consider and Approve Employment of Professional Personnel
**Context:** The board will receive a staffing update from the Human Resources department and vote to officially approve the hiring of professional staff members. This is a standard recurring item required by Texas law, as the board must formally approve employment of certain personnel.

**Historical Summary:** HR reports and professional personnel approvals appear as regular consent agenda items at every CISD board meeting.

---

### VI.H. Receive Financial Reports
**Context:** The board will review regular financial reports providing an update on the district's revenues, expenditures, and overall fiscal status. This is a routine transparency and oversight item required of Texas school boards.

**Historical Summary:** Financial reports are a standing item on CISD's monthly board meeting agendas.

---

### VI.I. Approval of the TCEQ Required Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan for the Conroe ISD Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant Facility
**Context:** The board will vote to approve a Water Conservation and Drought Contingency Plan required by the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) for the district's Moorhead/Caney Creek Water Plant Facility. Texas regulations require entities that operate water systems to maintain and periodically update these plans.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

---

## Human Resources (Section VII)

### VII.A. Recognize Principal for DAEP/JJAEP
**Context:** The board will formally recognize the principal serving the District Alternative Education Program (DAEP) and/or Juvenile Justice Alternative Education Program (JJAEP). These programs serve students who have been removed from traditional school settings, and this recognition highlights the leadership guiding those programs.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

---

## Administration (Section VIII)

### VIII.A. Reconsider and Approve the Mascot for Cartwright Junior High
**Context:** The board will revisit and take a formal vote on the official mascot for Cartwright Junior High School. The use of the word "reconsider" suggests this item may have been discussed or voted on previously and is being brought back for further action or a revised decision.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

---

### VIII.B. Receive Information Regarding Senate Bill 546 Requirement for Bus Seat Belts
**Context:** The board will receive a presentation or update on Senate Bill 546, a Texas law that may require the district to equip school buses with seat belts. This is an informational item to help the board understand the requirements, timeline, and potential cost implications for the district.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

---

### VIII.C. Consider and Possibly Adopt District's Virtual School Plan
**Context:** The board will consider formally adopting the district's plan for virtual (online) schooling. Texas law requires school districts to have an approved virtual school plan in place to offer or continue virtual learning options for students.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

---

### VIII.D. Receive Information Regarding District Staffing Plan
**Context:** The board will receive an informational presentation on the district's staffing plan, which may address teacher and staff hiring, allocation, and workforce needs for the coming school year. This is an informational item and does not require a vote at this meeting.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

---

## Teaching and Learning (Section IX)

### IX.A. Consider and Possibly Approve Library Materials in Accordance with Texas Education Code §33.026, School Board Policy EFB (Local)
**Context:** The board will vote on approving library materials for district schools, following the process established by Texas Education Code §33.026 and local policy EFB. This process governs how the district reviews and selects books and other materials available to students in school libraries.

**Historical Summary:** Library material approvals have become a recurring item at CISD board meetings, reflecting statewide legislative focus on school library content. No specific prior action details are available from the provided records.

---

### IX.B. Consider and Possibly Adopt District's Special Education Homecoming Project Plan
**Context:** The board will consider approving a "Homecoming Project Plan" for Special Education, which appears to be an initiative supporting the reintegration or transition of special education students into general education settings or their home campuses. This type of plan is consistent with federal and state requirements to serve students in the least restrictive environment.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

---

### IX.C. Receive Information Regarding The Texas Standard Character Education Program
**Context:** The board will receive an informational update on the Texas Standard Character Education Program, a state initiative that encourages schools to incorporate character development — such as integrity, responsibility, and citizenship — into their curricula and culture. No vote is required at this meeting.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.

---

## Business/Finance (Section X)

### X.A. Receive 2026–2027 Budget Information
**Context:** The board will receive an early presentation on the proposed budget for the 2026–2027 school year. This is an informational item that allows trustees to begin reviewing revenue projections, spending priorities, and any funding changes ahead of formal budget adoption later in the year.

**Historical Summary:** Budget planning presentations are a recurring item in the spring months at CISD board meetings, as the district prepares to adopt a new annual budget before the start of the fiscal year.

---

## Planning and Construction (Section XI)

### XI.A. Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for The Woodlands 9th Grade and College Park High School Renovations Project
**Context:** The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk (CMR) — a procurement method where a contractor is hired early in the design process and guarantees a maximum price — for renovation work at The Woodlands 9th Grade campus and College Park High School. This is a significant spending decision related to facility improvements, likely connected to a voter-approved bond program.

**Historical Summary:** Construction and renovation projects at multiple campuses have been an ongoing focus for CISD, consistent with bond program implementation. No specific prior action details for this project are available from the provided records.

---

### XI.B. Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for The Auxiliary Facility Renovations Project
**Context:** The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk for renovations to an auxiliary district facility. The specific facility is not named in the agenda, but this follows the same CMR procurement process used for other capital projects.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific project based on provided records.

---

### XI.C. Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for the Caney Creek High School Renovation and District Safety and Security Upgrades Project
**Context:** The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk for a combined project at Caney Creek High School that includes both facility renovations and district-wide safety and security upgrades. Security-related construction is a high-priority area for Texas school districts under state law.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific project based on provided records.

---

### XI.D. Consider and Approve the Selection of a Construction Manager-at-Risk for the Anderson and Glen Loch Elementary School Additions and Renovations Project
**Context:** The board will vote to select a Construction Manager-at-Risk for addition and renovation work at Anderson Elementary and Glen Loch Elementary schools. This type of project may address capacity needs, aging infrastructure, or program space requirements at the campuses.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific project based on provided records.

---

### XI.E. Consider and Approve Annexation of San Jacinto Elementary Site into MUD 111
**Context:** The board will vote on whether to annex the San Jacinto Elementary School property into Municipal Utility District (MUD) 111, and authorize the Superintendent to sign the necessary documents. Annexation into a MUD can affect how water, sewer, and other utility services are provided to the campus and may have financial implications for the district.

**Historical Summary:** No prior history available for this specific item based on provided records.
